# Caption variants — 2026-05-10-team-photo-5yr

## Variant 1 — Story

Five years ago today, this practice was me, one borrowed chair, and a kettle that only half-worked.

Now it's the six people in this photo — plus the hundred-odd patients who kept showing up while we figured out what we were doing.

Cake tastes better when you've earned it. Thanks to every patient and every team member who made this real.

*Platform fit:* Instagram (reads natural with photo)
*Tone:* warm, specific, first-person, no overclaiming

## Variant 2 — Community

The people who made Meadow Lane Dental what it is.

Five years. Six people in this photo tonight. Five of them have been with me for at least two years.

Running a practice isn't about the principal — it's about whether the team shows up for each other. Mine does.

Cake optional. Continuity of care, not optional.

*Platform fit:* LinkedIn (denser, slightly more professional register)
*Tone:* proud, not boastful, lands the tenure stat as the real headline

## Variant 3 — Behind-the-scenes

The photos you don't normally see: the team after closing, no uniforms, a slightly crooked cake.

Because if a dental practice's public face is perfectly staged but the reality behind it is a revolving door of staff, you're getting a chair from strangers every six months.

We stay because we like it here. You benefit because we stay.

*Platform fit:* Instagram (behind-the-scenes is native there)
*Tone:* warm, slightly dry, makes the "continuity of care" point indirectly

## Variant 4 — Question-hook

Five years in one spot.

What keeps a small team together in a profession where most move every two?

Here's ours: the patients we'd built relationships with deserved continuity. Every year we stayed, another year of history with them we'd have thrown away by leaving.

What keeps your team together, if it stays together?

*Platform fit:* LinkedIn (drives replies)
*Tone:* inviting, philosophical without being pretentious, question at end for engagement

---

## Notes for the client

Three of these (Variants 1, 3, 4) lean into continuity-of-care framing — the long-form content and Priya's positioning both emphasise this. Variant 2 is the most "professional" register for LinkedIn. Variant 1 is the softest / most personal for Instagram.

No hashtags added — Priya's voice profile doesn't use them. Add if you want.
